votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions, including the digit extraction, palindrome checking, and counting logic over the specified range.

The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions, including the digit extraction, palindrome checking, and counting logic over the specified range.

The Python code faithfully translates the Coq definitions. to_digits_helper iterates up to k times collecting digits in reverse order, is_palindrome_b checks if digits equal their reverse (excluding 0), count_in_range counts from 1 to k inclusive, and count_palindromes_impl returns the tuple of even and odd palindrome counts. The logic matches the Coq spec for all concrete positive integer inputs.

The Python code faithfully translates the Coq definitions. to_digits_helper iterates up to k times collecting digits in reverse order, is_palindrome_b checks if digits equal their reverse (excluding 0), count_in_range counts from 1 to k inclusive, and count_palindromes_impl returns the tuple of even and odd palindrome counts. The logic matches the Coq spec for all concrete positive integer inputs.

The Python code faithfully implements the Coq specification. Both use the same logic: to_digits_helper extracts digits in reverse order, is_palindrome_b checks if digits equal their reverse (excluding 0), is_even checks divisibility by 2, and count_in_range counts values from 1 to k satisfying a predicate. The count_palindromes_impl returns the same tuple of even and odd palindrome counts. All helper functions produce identical results on concrete inputs.
Python mirrors Coq’s digit extraction, palindrome (excluding 0), parity checks, counting over 1..n, and returns (even, odd) in the same order; precondition and spec checks match.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: same digit extraction (reversed), palindrome check with 0 excluded, even/odd tests, and counting over 1..n. The precondition n>0 and the spec equality are implemented identically. Therefore results match for all concrete inputs.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: same digit extraction (stopping at n=0), palindrome check excluding 0, even/odd checks, and counting over 1..n. The pair (even palindromes, odd palindromes) is computed identically for all positive n.
